build_check.description: Headless launch exits 0 without fatal errors; this 0/1 gate multiplies the rest.

requirements.description: The player aims siege weapons by adjusting angle and power with a drag or slider interface. A trajectory preview line shows the projected path before firing. 0 if there is no aiming mechanic, if angle/power cannot be adjusted, or if no trajectory preview exists. Full credit needs authored sprites/illustrated assets; shape/default-widget versions cap at 0.5.

Projectiles follow physics-based arcs (gravity, initial velocity) and impact castle structures. On impact, blocks take damage and can crack or break. Structural collapse occurs when support is removed — unsupported blocks fall. 0 if projectiles teleport, if there is no physics arc, or if structures are indestructible. Full credit needs authored sprites/illustrated assets; shape/default-widget versions cap at 0.5.

The player has a limited number of shots per level. A shot counter is visible and decreases with each fire. Running out of shots before meeting the destruction threshold fails the level. 0 if shots are unlimited or if no shot counter exists. Full credit needs authored sprites/illustrated assets; shape/default-widget versions cap at 0.5.

A destruction percentage meter tracks how much of the castle has been destroyed. Meeting a clear threshold completes the level. The meter updates in real-time as blocks break. 0 if there is no destruction tracking or if the level has no completion condition. Full credit needs authored sprites/illustrated assets; shape/default-widget versions cap at 0.5.

The full loop connects: title -> level select -> aim/fire gameplay -> victory/defeat -> stars earned -> next level or retry. Completed levels unlock new ones without relaunch. 0 if any transition is missing, progression is absent, or result flow is scripted. Full credit needs authored sprites/illustrated assets.

At least three weapon types exist (trebuchet, ballista, catapult) with different projectile arcs, speeds, blast radii, and visual appearances. The player selects weapon type before firing. 0 if only one weapon exists or if types are functionally identical. Full credit needs distinct authored art; shape/color/text-only variants cap at 0.5.

Castle structures use different materials (wood, stone, iron) with different durability. Wood breaks easily, stone requires multiple hits, iron resists most weapons. 0 if all blocks have identical durability or if material type has no effect. Full credit needs distinct authored art; shape/color/text-only variants cap at 0.5.

A campaign has at least five selectable, playable levels with increasingly complex castles, varied materials, and persistent unlocks. 0 if there is only one level, no complexity increase, repeated templates, or menu-only levels. Full credit needs distinct authored art.

Later levels add environmental challenges such as wind that changes trajectories or terrain obstacles that block shots, with HUD cues and counterplay. 0 if conditions are identical, wind is decorative text, or obstacles are passive scenery. Full credit needs distinct authored art.

A star rating rewards efficient destruction from actual shots used and destruction percent; three stars require optimal play and persist on level select. 0 if ratings are absent, not performance-based, or statically awarded. Full credit needs distinct authored art.

The HUD shows shots, destruction percent, weapon type, and wind using themed icons/gauges in non-overlapping regions. 0 if shots, destruction, or weapon info is missing, or if readouts are plain text/debug labels. Full credit needs themed UI with real assets.

The scene clearly shows the siege weapon on the left, the castle on the right, and terrain between them. Castle blocks are distinguishable by material. The trajectory preview is visible when aiming. 0 if weapon and castle are indistinguishable or if materials look identical. Full credit needs themed UI with real assets; plain text/unstyled/raw-shape readability caps at 0.5.

Actions produce visible feedback: firing shows the weapon animate, projectiles arc visibly, impacts produce dust/debris particles, blocks crack and fall with physics, and destruction meter updates. 0 if firing produces no visual effect or if blocks disappear instantly. Full credit needs themed UI with real assets; plain text/unstyled/raw-shape readability caps at 0.5.

Overall art coherence: siege weapons, castles, terrain, projectiles, UI, menus, results, and effects share a medieval-engineering visual language. 0 if styles clash, read as default Godot grey, or rely on mixed placeholders/programmatic shapes.

Siege weapon sprites are real illustrated or pixel-art machines (wooden frames, ropes, counterweights). Castle blocks use textured stone, wood, and iron materials. 0 if weapons or blocks are plain colored shapes. Programmatic shapes/solid fills/untextured geometry cap at 0.5.

The background terrain uses real illustrated landscape (hills, grass, sky) with depth and atmosphere. The ground between weapon and castle has surface detail. 0 if the background is a flat color fill. Programmatic shapes/solid fills/untextured geometry cap at 0.5.

HUD and UI use styled themed widgets: shot counter has medieval frame, destruction meter has themed bar, weapon selector has illustrated icons, and level select has castle thumbnails. 0 if UI elements are unstyled Godot defaults. Programmatic shapes/solid fills/untextured geometry cap at 0.5.

Animation and atmosphere: weapon firing animates (arm swings, rope snaps), projectiles spin in flight, impacts produce dust clouds and debris, blocks tumble with physics, and the scene has ambient atmosphere (drifting clouds, birds). 0 if all events snap instantly. Programmatic shapes/solid fills/untextured geometry cap at 0.5.
